title: Strobilanthes baracatanensis
taxonomy: Strobilanthes baracatanensis belongs to the family Acanthaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus or achyranthes family. This family consists of approximately 2,964 species of plants, many of which are shrubs, trees, or herbaceous perennials. Strobilanthes baracatanensis is a species of flowering plant in the genus Strobilanthes, which comprises around 350 species of plants. The genus Strobilanthes is further classified as belonging to the order Lamiales and the clade asterids. The classification of Strobilanthes baracatanensis reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
uses: Strobilanthes baracatanensis has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, sore throat, and respiratory issues. Its leaves and stems are used to prepare teas or infusions that are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. In some cultures, the plant is also used to treat digestive problems and as a natural remedy for colds and flu. Additionally, Strobilanthes baracatanensis has been used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these uses and to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties.

intro: Strobilanthes baracatanensis, commonly known as the Philippine blue evergreen or blue mountain, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Acanthaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, boasting various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of S. baracatanensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used to make herbal teas, such as "tisanes," to harness its health benefits.
